# @zakkster/lite-await

Zero-GC bridge between @zakkster/lite-signal and Promise / async-await. Wait
for signal values, race multi-source predicates, AbortSignal-first
cancellation, bidirectional promise<->signal conversion. The missing
async-coordination primitive for the lite-signal ecosystem.

ESM only. Node >=18. Peer dep: @zakkster/lite-signal ^1.2.0. Zero runtime deps.

## Public API

import {
    whenSignal, allOf, anyOf, raceOf,
    withTimeout, withAbort,
    whenTruthy, whenEquals,
    fromPromise,
    whenStatechart,
    TimeoutError
} from "@zakkster/lite-await";

Every awaiter accepts: { timeout?: number, signal?: AbortSignal }
- timeout (ms): rejects with TimeoutError if no settlement in time.
- signal: rejects with AbortError if the signal aborts before settlement.

### Core primitives

whenSignal(source, predicate, opts?) -> Promise<T>
  Resolve when source() first satisfies predicate(value). If already true on
  first read, resolves on next microtask. Settlement (resolve / abort /
  timeout) cleans the internal effect.

allOf(specs, opts?) -> Promise<T[]>
  specs: Array<[source, predicate]>. Resolves with values in input order when
  every spec satisfies. Any rejection aborts the remaining specs.

anyOf(specs, opts?) -> Promise<{ index, value }>
  First spec to resolve wins; siblings aborted. Rejects with AggregateError
  ONLY if every spec rejects independently. Bundle abort surfaces AbortError
  directly.

raceOf(specs, opts?) -> Promise<{ index, value }>
  First spec to settle wins -- success OR failure cascades. Use for
  success-vs-error race patterns.

### Promise wrappers (non-signal-aware)

withTimeout(promise, ms) -> Promise<T>
  Wrap arbitrary Promise with deadline. Rejects TimeoutError. Inner promise
  is NOT cancelled (arbitrary promises aren't AbortSignal-aware). For
  signal-based work, use the primitive's `timeout` option instead.

withAbort(promise, signal) -> Promise<T>
  Wrap arbitrary Promise with AbortSignal. Same caveat: inner work continues.

### Convenience

whenTruthy(source, opts?)      // whenSignal(source, Boolean, opts)
whenEquals(source, target, opts?)  // whenSignal(source, (v) => Object.is(v, target), opts)

### Bidirectional bridge

fromPromise(promise, initialData?) -> Signal<AsyncState<T>>
  AsyncState<T> = { status, data, error }, status in
  "pending" | "resolved" | "rejected". Updates EXACTLY ONCE on settlement.
  Dispose via lite-signal's `dispose(sig)` when done.

### lite-statechart specialization

whenStatechart(machine, stateName, opts?) -> Promise<void>
  Hooks into machine.onTransition directly (no tracking effect node).
  Duck-typed: any { state: { peek() }, onTransition(fn): () => void } works.
  Resolves on next microtask if already in target state.

### Errors

TimeoutError extends Error
  { name: "TimeoutError", timeout: number }

Abort errors are platform-shaped: signal.reason if set, else
DOMException("Aborted", "AbortError"), else Error with name "AbortError".

## Design invariants

- ASCII-only source. ESM only. Zero runtime dependencies.
- Cleanup is STRUCTURAL: every settlement path (resolve / reject / timeout /
  abort) runs the same fullCleanup that disposes the effect, clears timeout,
  removes abort listener. There is no path where one is skipped.
- A throwing predicate or source() getter settles by REJECTION: the thrown
  value rejects the promise and the effect tears down, on both the sync first
  read and later change-driven fires. The throw never escapes at the signal
  writer's .set() call site.
- Pre-aborted signal short-circuits every primitive: returns rejected Promise
  synchronously, never constructs internal machinery to tear it down.
- Hoisted untrack body in whenSignal -- checkPredicate is allocated once at
  registration, reused per fire (zero per-change allocation).
- Late-binding stopFn pattern: handles the race where effect() fires its body
  synchronously on first registration and the predicate is already satisfied
  (mirrors WatchEx.js's watchUntil).
- Internal AbortController per combinator coordinates cleanup across child
  whenSignal calls. linkUserSignal forwards user's signal in and cleans up
  the forward listener on settlement.

## Performance

Honest numbers (Node 22, M1-class machine, after warmup):
- whenSignal resolve:        213K ops/s, ~4 B/op retained
- whenSignal pre-aborted:     87K ops/s, ~3 B/op
- allOf 4-spec:              103K ops/s, ~5 B/op
- anyOf 4-spec:               55K ops/s, ~3 B/op
- raceOf 4-spec:              60K ops/s, ~3 B/op
- fromPromise lifecycle:     228K ops/s, ~5 B/op

Final lite-signal pool returns to baseline (zero retained nodes) after each
scenario. 4096-cycle leak probe in test/09-cleanup-leak.test.mjs.

## Common patterns

1. Wait for ready with deadline:
   await whenSignal(state, (s) => s === "live", { timeout: 5000 });

2. Wait for ALL of several conditions:
   await allOf([[jwt, j => j !== null], [room, r => r === "joined"]]);

3. First-to-resolve from N sources:
   const { index, value } = await anyOf(specs);

4. Success-vs-error race (EBS / API pattern):
   const r = await raceOf([
       [response, (r) => r?.id === id],
       [error,    (e) => e?.id === id]
   ], { timeout: 10000 });
   if (r.index === 1) throw new ApiError(r.value);

5. Project a fetch into a signal:
   const userQuery = fromPromise(fetchUser(id));
   effect(() => {
       const s = userQuery();
       if (s.status === "resolved") render(s.data);
   });

6. Cancel everything on dispose:
   const ctrl = new AbortController();
   const p = whenSignal(sig, pred, { signal: ctrl.signal });
   // ... later ...
   ctrl.abort();   // p rejects with AbortError; effect cleaned up

## Anti-patterns

- DO NOT use lite-await inside per-frame render loops. It is for one-shot
  async coordination at lifecycle boundaries. For per-frame work use signals
  + effect().
- DO NOT expect withTimeout / withAbort to cancel arbitrary inner work. They
  cannot; the inner Promise has no AbortSignal awareness. Pass the signal /
  timeout DIRECTLY to the primitive that creates the inner work.
- DO NOT allocate a fresh predicate closure on every call site. Hoist common
  predicates as module-level constants when possible.
- DO NOT forget to dispose() signals you create. The library cleans up its
  own effects, but signals you allocate are your responsibility.
